text.title: Outcome Based Roadmaps Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Outcome Based Roadmaps. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every outcome must follow the pattern verb+metric+delta+timeframe; outcomes missing any component are invalid.
rule.rationale: Every outcome must follow the pattern verb+metric+delta+timeframe; outcomes missing any component are invalid.

rule.statement: Cap quarterly outcomes at 3 for solo, 5 for small team; more destroys focus.
rule.rationale: Cap quarterly outcomes at 3 for solo, 5 for small team; more destroys focus.

rule.statement: Pair every outcome with a falsifying kill signal: 'if metric < X by week N, kill this bet'.
rule.rationale: Pair every outcome with a falsifying kill signal: 'if metric < X by week N, kill this bet'.

rule.statement: Tie every outcome to an existing analytics dashboard URL before publishing.
rule.rationale: Tie every outcome to an existing analytics dashboard URL before publishing.

rule.statement: Outcomes are frozen for the quarter; only the candidate-solution list mutates.
rule.rationale: Outcomes are frozen for the quarter; only the candidate-solution list mutates.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-outcome-based-roadmaps.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
